_meta.source: data-engineer coordinator, hand-curated from 12-batch corpus parse 2026-06-04
_meta.purpose: Maps Meesho XLSX field-name variants to a canonical internal name. Backend seeds `field_aliases` table from this. Frontend renders canonical names. XLSX exporter reverses canonical → original (per category) for round-trip compatibility.
notes: Many entries are tentative — consolidate after founder review.
When the same canonical maps to multiple Meesho variants, the XLSX exporter must know which variant to use for which category. That mapping is in `data/parsed/batch_NN_*.json` (field name per leaf).
Typos like 'Primiary'/'Seconadry' are preserved verbatim in the variant list because Meesho's XLSX upload validator uses exact-match strings.

color.note: Color/Colour are spelling variants. Bottom Color, Primary/Secondary Colour are positional variants for multi-color products — keep distinct canonical names IF positional semantics matter to frontend (likely yes).

battery_presence.note: All these convey 'does this product need/include batteries'. 8 variants found in B3+B4+B5+B11+B12.

compatible_devices.note: 4 variants in B4 (Consumer Electronics) for the same concept. Compatible Models is the most common; max enum 4,481 (largest in corpus).

brand.note: Lowercase/plural variants found in B5 (Home & Kitchen).

manufacturer_compliance.note: 3,771/3,772 leaves use the 3-field block. Eye-Serum (leaf 12378) uses the combined single-field 'Manufacturer Details'. Backend stores both representations on seller_profile.

importer_compliance.note: Same pattern. Often optional in B11 (Books/Auto/Pet/Bags).

net_weight.note: Eye-Serum uses 'Product weight (gms)' (lowercase 'w'). Some Home leaves use 'Product Net Weight'.

primary_camera_count.note: Meesho typo preserved verbatim — 'Primiary' instead of 'Primary'. XLSX exporter MUST use the typo for round-trip.

assembly_required.note: B3 (Kids & Toys) — 2 variants for same concept.

warranty_family.note: Warranty is not a single field — it's a family of related fields with different semantics. Don't collapse them; keep each as its own canonical and render them as a coordinated group in the wizard 'Warranty' step (conditional for Electronics/Appliances).

license_cosmetic.note: Used in Beauty & Health (B10), some Home & Kitchen appliances (B5). Optional unless seller has licensed product.

fssai.note: COMPULSORY in 321/321 Grocery leaves (B7). Optional in 15-16 Beauty/Pet leaves where there's food overlap. This is the strongest Onboarding extension signal in the corpus.

bis_isi.note: Kids & Toys (28 leaves), Consumer Electronics (20), Appliances (5). Always optional. Seller-specific.

isbn.note: Books (163 leaves). Optional — Meesho is lenient. Founder question: enforce stricter than Meesho?

wireless_certification.note: Indian wireless/safety certifications. R Number (106 leaves in B4), IS Number (112), CM/L (26). All optional. Onboarding extension for Electronics.

19_36_37_14_88_34.note: Required if the seller has products that require AYUSH or cosmetic licensing. Optional otherwise.

80.note: Meesho is surprisingly lenient on Books. Founder question pending.

75.note: Reuses FSSAI from Grocery for pet food products.
